title: Outdoor Event Security
description: Additive module for physical security considerations specific to temporary outdoor events, including perimeter control, crowd management, temporary structure security, and emergency coordination.

module_questions.text: Are event perimeter access points monitored and controlled to prevent unauthorized entry?
Is temporary fencing secured against tampering or unauthorized removal at event perimeter boundaries?
Are temporary structures such as stages and equipment areas secured against unauthorized access?
Are vendor areas secured with access controls to prevent unauthorized entry to equipment and supplies?
Is adequate lighting provided in parking areas and perimeter zones to support visibility and deter criminal activity?
Are crowd areas monitored to detect security incidents and enable timely response?
Are emergency egress routes clearly marked and maintained unobstructed during events?
Are roles and responsibilities defined for coordinating physical security response between security personnel and event staff?
Is power distribution equipment for temporary structures secured against physical tampering?

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Establish controlled access points at event perimeter with monitoring and verification procedures. Addresses: Uncontrolled access points can allow unauthorized entry and potential threats.
Implement perimeter monitoring and detection capabilities to identify breaches promptly. Addresses: Perimeter breaches can compromise event security if not detected and responded to promptly.

source_locator.source: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide

sources.label: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide (2020)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Secure temporary fencing against tampering or unauthorized removal through proper installation and monitoring. Addresses: Temporary fencing may be vulnerable to tampering or unauthorized removal.

source_locator.source: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide

sources.label: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide (2020)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Implement physical security measures for temporary structures to prevent unauthorized access to critical systems. Addresses: Temporary structures require physical security measures to prevent unauthorized access to critical systems.

sources.label: NFPA 101 - Life Safety Code (2021) - Temporary Structures

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Provide adequate lighting in parking areas and perimeter zones to improve visibility and deter criminal activity. Addresses: Insufficient lighting in parking and perimeter areas increases vulnerability to criminal activity.

Establish monitoring capabilities for crowd areas to detect security incidents and enable timely response. Addresses: Insufficient visibility and monitoring in crowd areas may allow security incidents to go undetected.

source_locator.source: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide

sources.label: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide (2022)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Maintain clear and unobstructed emergency egress routes with adequate signage. Addresses: Unclear or obstructed emergency egress routes can delay evacuation during incidents.

Establish coordination procedures between security personnel and event staff to prevent security gaps. Addresses: Insufficient coordination between security personnel and event staff can create security gaps.

source_locator.source: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide

sources.label: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide (2022)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Implement access controls for vendor areas to protect equipment and supplies. Addresses: Vendor areas without access controls may be vulnerable to theft or tampering.

source_locator.source: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide

sources.label: Event Safety Alliance - Event Safety Guide (2022)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Secure power distribution equipment with physical protection measures to prevent tampering. Addresses: Power distribution equipment requires physical protection to prevent tampering.

sources.label: NFPA 101 - Life Safety Code (2021) - Temporary Structures

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Implement vehicle access control measures to prevent vehicle-borne threats at event perimeters. Addresses: Vehicle access control is critical to prevent vehicle-borne threats.

source_locator.source: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide

sources.label: DHS - Special Events Security Planning Guide (2020)
